definition.name: irreducible_representation
definition.definition: A *-representation π : A → B(H) of a *-algebra A on a Hilbert space H is called irreducible if the only closed subspaces of H that are invariant under all operators π(a) (a ∈ A) are {0} and H itself. By Schur's lemma, π is irreducible if and only if its commutant is trivial: π(A)' = C·I (scalar multiples of the identity). For a state φ ∈ S(A), the GNS representation π_φ is irreducible if and only if φ is a pure state (an extreme point of S(A)). Two irreducible representations π, σ are unitarily equivalent (written π ≅ σ) if there exists a unitary U : H_π → H_σ with Uπ(a) = σ(a)U for all a ∈ A. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.9.
